input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known breaking change — `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` in API 30+. In .NET 10 (MAUI), the recommended approach changed.

Let me provide a concise, accurate answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI evolution.

In .NET 10 / MAUI, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was removed because Android deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` starting with API 30. 

The newer recommended approaches involve using edge-to-edge display with proper `WindowInsets` handling, or leveraging the new `KeyboardAutoScroll` behavior that MAUI introduced to handle keyboard visibility more gracefully across different Android versions. Use `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` to let MAUI handle scrolling automatically when the keyboard appears, or wrap content in a `ScrollView` for the same effect. On Android specifically, I can set the soft input mode in the manifest, though the resize approach is deprecated—the real issue is that .NET 10 likely defaults to edge-to-edge rendering, which breaks the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` behavior, so I need to handle window insets properly instead. I need to focus on how `WindowInsets` handling works in .NET 10 to properly address the keyboard overlap issue on modern Android versions.
